behaviors.description: Required patient fields (name, date of birth, insurance ID) are validated before any downstream processing and missing fields produce a clear error listing all missing items.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is validator-to-orchestrator handoff: must prove downstream modules are not called when fields are missing and that aggregated errors are returned. Higher levels are disqualified because behavior text is only about pre-check gating and error content, not OS/network/AI.
behaviors.test_description: Wire intake orchestrator with real validation module and stubbed insurance/triage/scheduling/confirmation services. Submit payload missing multiple required fields; assert error lists all missing fields and assert downstream service stubs were not invoked.

behaviors.description: Date of birth validation rejects future dates, dates more than 150 years ago, and non-date strings.
behaviors.justification: Pure local validation logic with no module boundary requirement in behavior text. Higher levels are disqualified because no downstream interaction, OS/network/subprocess, or AI dependency is described.
behaviors.test_description: Test DOB validator function directly with table-driven cases: future date, >150-year-old date, malformed string, and valid boundary dates.

behaviors.description: The insurance eligibility check sends the correct patient ID and plan code to the payer API and correctly interprets `eligible` vs `not eligible` vs `pending`.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is API client plumbing plus status mapping across modules. IA-UA demotion proof: exact text checks request fields and interpretation only ("sends the correct patient ID and plan code" / "correctly interprets eligible vs not eligible vs pending"), not model-output quality. System is rejected because a local stub server/client can reproduce this failure boundary without real external network.
behaviors.test_description: Run insurance service against a local stub payer endpoint (or injected transport fake) that records request payload and returns each status variant. Assert outgoing patient ID/plan code and mapped internal eligibility states.

behaviors.description: When the payer API is unreachable, the system marks eligibility as `pending` and continues the intake rather than blocking.
behaviors.justification: Failure is error propagation and fallback behavior between insurance module and intake orchestration. System rejected because unreachable can be represented by deterministic transport failure in-process; behavior does not require validating real network stack characteristics.
behaviors.test_description: Inject payer client failure (timeout/connection error exception) in intake flow. Assert eligibility becomes pending and subsequent stages (triage/scheduling/confirmation) still execute.

behaviors.description: Triage invocation includes the reported symptoms and configured emergency rubric in the model input payload.
behaviors.justification: This is LLM-call plumbing, not LLM judgment. IA-UA demotion proof from behavior text: it asserts payload inclusion only ("includes ... in the model input payload"), not triage quality. Agentic rejected because no assertion on model decision correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Execute triage service with a mock model gateway that captures request payload. Assert symptoms and emergency rubric fields are present and correctly formatted in the prompt/input body.

behaviors.description: The live AI symptom triage assigns high urgency to symptom combinations that indicate emergency conditions (for example chest pain plus shortness of breath).
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model judgment quality for symptom combinations; deterministic stubs would hide failures. Workflow rejected because this behavior concerns a single model decision, not multiple agentic steps in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run repeated live triage calls on emergency symptom cases and assert urgency is high (with tolerance criteria across runs), tracking misclassification rate against an acceptance threshold.

behaviors.description: The triage result parser stores a clinician-readable explanation field (not only a numeric score).
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at parser-to-persistence boundary: explanation may be dropped/truncated when stored. Agentic rejected because behavior checks structural field persistence, not explanation quality.
behaviors.test_description: Feed representative triage result payload into parsing/storage path with test DB/repository. Assert persisted record contains non-empty explanation text alongside numeric urgency.

behaviors.description: The live AI symptom triage explanation cites case-specific symptoms and urgency rationale, not generic boilerplate.
behaviors.justification: Behavior directly evaluates semantic quality of generated explanation from live AI output; mocks would always pass and miss real failures. Workflow rejected because this is one agentic generation step, not multi-agentic sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Call live triage on varied symptom inputs; evaluate explanation for explicit mention of input symptoms and concrete urgency rationale using rubric-based checks, and fail generic/template-only outputs.

behaviors.description: An appointment is scheduled in the first available slot that matches the assigned urgency level: high urgency gets same-day, medium gets within 48 hours.
behaviors.justification: Requires scheduling logic plus shared calendar data interaction; local DB/fixtures are sufficient. System rejected because behavior does not require OS/network primitives beyond local module+DB wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Populate test calendar DB with controlled availability. Invoke scheduler for high and medium urgency cases; assert chosen slot is first valid one and satisfies same-day / <=48h constraints.

behaviors.description: The scheduling step does not double-book a time slot even when two patients are processed concurrently.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is concurrency control at application+DB transaction boundary; this is beyond unit logic but can be caught with integrated local DB concurrency test. System rejected because no external OS/network/subprocess dependency is required beyond local transactional behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run two concurrent scheduling requests targeting overlapping availability against the real scheduling module and test DB with transactional constraints. Assert exactly one booking succeeds for the contested slot.

behaviors.description: The confirmation message includes patient name, appointment time, provider name, and preparation instructions appropriate to visit type.
behaviors.justification: Behavior spans data handoff from appointment/visit-type context into message rendering/template selection. Higher levels are disqualified because no real OS/network/AI quality dependency is specified.
behaviors.test_description: Execute confirmation composition with realistic appointment + visit-type inputs through message service. Assert output includes required fields and visit-type-specific preparation instructions.

behaviors.description: Pipeline orchestration (validate, insurance, triage, schedule, confirm) completes correctly with deterministic stubbed triage output.
behaviors.justification: This explicitly targets orchestration across modules with deterministic stubbed triage, so failures are module handoff/state sequencing bugs. IA-UA demotion proof: exact text says "deterministic stubbed triage output," disqualifying Agentic/Workflow requirements tied to real model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end intake orchestration in-process with stubbed triage and controllable insurance/scheduling dependencies. Assert each stage executes in order, state is handed off correctly, and final confirmation is produced.

behaviors.description: The full live intake pipeline (validate, insurance, triage, schedule, confirm) completes for a real patient submission without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: Includes live AI triage call inside full pipeline; real-model behavior can affect completion and cannot be validated with stubs alone. Workflow considered but rejected because behavior specifies one AI decision point, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Execute a production-like end-to-end run with real patient-like submission, live payer API path, live AI triage, and real scheduling/confirmation integrations; assert autonomous completion and terminal success artifacts.
